activeRepositoryJudgmentTitle: Repository documentation identifies actionable follow-up work from: Add backlog quality

candidates.title: Implement Focused Improvement Loop UX
candidates.evidence: docs/repository-improvement-product-redesign.md — explicit product vision: "Open Agent IDE → see the next best improvement → generate one prompt → implement → validate → refresh → repeat"
candidates.whyItMatters: Transforms Agent IDE from a repository-intelligence browser into a focused improvement operating system. Every user visit answers one question: what is the single highest-impact improvement and what happens next?
candidates.whyOutranks: Addresses the primary user journey end-to-end. All other product opportunities are components or downstream effects of this loop.

candidates.title: Auto-compose Single Implementation Prompt from All Intelligence
candidates.evidence: docs/repository-improvement-product-redesign.md — "Agent IDE composes one complete implementation prompt from all internal intelligence."
candidates.whyItMatters: Users never need to know what a context package, builder prompt, or architect prompt is. One complete prompt encapsulates all intelligence needed to execute the improvement.
candidates.whyOutranks: Directly reduces the cognitive burden of the improvement loop. Lower-ranked candidates improve subsystems rather than the central user interaction.

candidates.title: Validate Product Bet: The repository is currently focused on making repositor
candidates.evidence: .ai/strategy.md — Current Product Bet: "The repository is currently focused on making repository understanding the pr..."
candidates.whyItMatters: The active product bet "The repository is currently focused on making repository understanding the pr..." needs validation evidence. A focused experiment or instrumentation pass confirms or refutes the bet before further investment.
candidates.whyOutranks: Validating the active product bet has higher leverage than any individual feature — it gates the entire product direction.

candidates.title: Instrument and Validate Current Focus: The repository is currently focused on making repository understanding the pr...
candidates.evidence: .ai/goals.md — Current Focus: "The repository is currently focused on making repository understanding the pr..."
candidates.whyItMatters: The declared Current Focus is "The repository is currently focused on making repository understanding the pr...". Ensuring this focus is instrumented and measurable against the North Star Metric maximizes product signal per development cycle.
candidates.whyOutranks: Current Focus alignment has higher strategic score than backlog items because it directly addresses the declared product priority.

candidates.title: Collapse Primary Navigation to Improvement Loop Only
candidates.evidence: docs/repository-improvement-product-redesign.md — "Screens that should be removed from normal usage" and "UI elements that should become Advanced"
candidates.whyItMatters: Eliminates decision fatigue. The owner sees one recommendation, one rationale, one button. Repository Health, Context Package, Validation, Verification, and Prompt Center move to Advanced.
candidates.whyOutranks: Reducing navigation complexity has higher product leverage than adding features to existing surfaces.

candidates.title: Instrument North Star Metric: Repository handoff readiness is Ready with high canonic
candidates.evidence: .ai/goals.md — North Star Metric: "Repository handoff readiness is Ready with high canonical intelligence consis..."
candidates.whyItMatters: The declared North Star is "Repository handoff readiness is Ready with high canonical intelligence consis...". Verifying this metric is instrumented and tracked in production ensures the product direction is data-driven.
candidates.whyOutranks: An uninstrumented North Star Metric means all product judgment is qualitative — instrumenting it enables quantitative prioritization.

candidates.title: Complete and Evaluate Current Experiment: Can the system reliably deliver the current focus:
candidates.evidence: .ai/strategy.md — Current Experiment: "Can the system reliably deliver the current focus: The repository is currentl..."
candidates.whyItMatters: An active experiment "Can the system reliably deliver the current focus: The repository is currentl..." is underway. Reaching a clear go/no-go decision on this experiment unlocks the next product bet and prevents investment in the wrong direction.
candidates.whyOutranks: Active experiments have time-value — delayed evaluation reduces signal quality and wastes development cycles.

candidates.title: Automate Post-Implementation Validation and Refresh
candidates.evidence: docs/repository-improvement-product-redesign.md — "Validation & Refresh — automatically handle the post-implementation loop"
candidates.whyItMatters: After implementation, the owner clicks one button. Validation, refresh, and next-improvement selection happen automatically. The loop closes without manual steps.
candidates.whyOutranks: Automates the most repetitive part of the improvement loop. Backlog-only items below this threshold do not change the core loop.

candidates.title: Verify Success Criteria Are Measurable and Tracked
candidates.evidence: .ai/goals.md — Success Criteria: "Control Plane reports repository handoff readiness as Ready."
candidates.whyItMatters: 2 success criteria are defined. A verification pass confirms each is tracked, measurable, and tied to observable product behavior — not just intentions.
candidates.whyOutranks: Defined but untracked success criteria produce false confidence. Verification is low-cost with high strategic impact.
